image:image.image:title: Chicken Yassa (Authentic Senegalese Recipe, 30 min)
image:image.image:caption: Senegalese chicken yassa: marinated in lemon and mustard, grilled, then simmered with deeply caramelized onions. Ready in 30 minutes.

Mafé (West African Peanut Stew with Chicken)
image:image.image:caption: Mafé: West African peanut butter stew with chicken, tomato and root vegetables. A rich, creamy one-pot meal from Mali and Senegal.

Alloco (Ivorian Fried Plantains, Street Food Classic)
image:image.image:caption: Alloco: ripe plantains fried until caramelized. The iconic Ivorian street food, ready in 15 minutes. Serve with spicy onion sauce.

Thiéboudienne (Senegalese National Dish, Rice & Fish)
image:image.image:caption: Thiéboudienne: Senegal's national dish. Fish-stuffed and simmered with vegetables in a rich tomato sauce, served over red jollof-style rice.

image:image.image:caption: Moroccan chicken tagine: slow-cooked chicken with preserved lemon, green olives, saffron and ginger. A fragrant North African classic.

Ndolé (Cameroonian National Dish with Bitter Leaves and Peanuts)
image:image.image:caption: Ndolé: Cameroon's national dish. Bitter leaves simmered with ground peanuts, beef and shrimp. Rich, nutty, complex.

image:image.image:caption: Recipe authentic of the ceebu jen white Senegalese : rice parfumé with broth of fish and vegetables fresh. A dish familial savory.

image:image.image:caption: The version red of the national dish Senegalese. The rice is cooked in a tomato sauce rich with of the fish and some vegetables.

image:image.image:caption: Suya: Nigerian grilled beef skewers crusted in spicy peanut rub (yaji). The ultimate West African street food, ready in 25 minutes.

image:image.image:caption: Wolof-style jollof rice: broken rice cooked in rich tomato-pepper sauce with smoky depth. The ancestor of all West African jollof.
